nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Intermediate Russian I
nodes.description: Continued practical work in all language skills (speaking, listening, reading, writing), with more analysis of the structure of Russian. Continuation of cultural components. Prerequisite: RUSS 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: The fourth meeting of this course is asynchronous online; Open to degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Intermediate Russian II
nodes.description: Continued practical work in all language skills (speaking, listening, reading, writing), with more analysis of the structure of Russian. Continuation of cultural components. Prerequisite: RUSS 2100.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Elementary Russian II
nodes.description: An introduction to all aspects of contemporary standard Russian: speaking, listening, reading, writing. Cultural components include topics such as music, art, literature, and current events. Prerequisite: RUSS 1100 or equivalent.
nodes.other_requirements: 4th mtg Tuesday meeting online asynchronous; Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Culture/Civ 20/21st Centuries
nodes.description: Social, cultural, and political institutions from the 1905 revolution to the present. Particular attention to tensions between official and unofficial culture during the Soviet period. Prerequisite: RUSS 2200.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Russian Through Film
nodes.description: See schedule of courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: IS:Russian Romantic Poetry
nodes.description: A course which is tailored to fit the interests of a specific student, which occurs outside the traditional classroom/laboratory setting under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Russian Roots
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students by permission and override. This course is designed for learners at the Novice High to Intermediate level and higher (based on the ACTFL proficiency scale). You should already have some foundation in basic Russian grammar, including verb conjugation, case endings, a beginner’s vocabulary, basic understanding of how words are formed.

nodes.title: Performing Russian
nodes.description: Practical work on Russian intonation and the development of lexicon and fluency (vocabulary building, communicative strategies) using primarily Russian materials. Prerequisite: RUSS 2100. Pre/Co-requisite: RUSS 2200.

nodes.title: Elementary Russian I
nodes.description: An introduction to all aspects of contemporary standard Russian: speaking, listening, reading, writing. Cultural components include topics such as music, art, literature, and current events. No previous knowledge of Russian needed for RUSS 1100.
nodes.other_requirements: If you have studied or spoken Russian before, even if it wasn’t for very long, speak with or email me to arrange a placement conference. If you grew up in a home where Russian was spoken, you must have a placement conference, even if you do not know how to read or write it yet.
